basic_info.name: Lin Xiaoyun
basic_info.education: Master's degree from the School of Architecture and Art, China Academy of Art

music_background.level: Amateur Grade 10 (certified by the Chinese Musicians' Association), capable of improvisational accompaniment
music_background.learning_history: Started piano启蒙 at age 5 at the Hangzhou Children's Palace under a retired professor from the Affiliated High School of Zhejiang Conservatory of Music. Attended weekly lessons consistently from elementary through high school without interruption. Although not a music major in university, she continued self-studying music theory and composition fundamentals.
music_background.current_practice: Currently practicing Debussy's 'Clair de Lune' to capture its hazy tonal quality, while also experimenting with creating simple ambient music demos for her architectural design projects.

family.name: Lin Jianguo
family.music_connection: A retired middle school Chinese literature teacher with a deep love for traditional Chinese folk music. In his youth, he was an accomplished erhu player and often told young Xiaoyun stories behind 'The Moon Reflected in the Second Spring,' serving as her启蒙 into the aesthetic beauty of Chinese melodies.

family.name: Chen Xiuying
family.music_connection: Formerly a textile factory worker who was a lead singer in the factory's choir performances during her youth. She saved diligently to buy the family's first Xinghai brand upright piano, firmly believing that music could refine one's character. She has been Xiaoyun's most steadfast supporter (and motivator) throughout her piano journey.

family.name: Lin Zihao
family.music_connection: Born after 2005, currently a high school student and electric guitar enthusiast who plays in a rock band. He frequently debates with Xiaoyun on whether 'classical music is outdated.' Occasionally, they attempt duets combining piano and electric guitar, resulting in fascinating stylistic collisions.

social_circle.name: Professor Zhang
social_circle.music_connection: Former associate professor in the Piano Department at Zhejiang Conservatory of Music and Xiaoyun's启蒙 teacher. Now retired, Xiaoyun visits him every two months for a lesson. Professor Zhang often critiques her, saying, 'Your technique is sufficient, but your emotion is lacking.'

social_circle.name: Su Su
social_circle.music_connection: An independent illustrator who doesn't play any instruments but possesses exceptional taste in record collecting. They often drink wine and listen to vinyl records together in their rented apartment; Su Su selects the tracks while Xiaoyun explains the background of each piece.

social_circle.name: Ah Jie
social_circle.music_connection: A sound designer at their firm. They met through work collaboration and frequently discuss the pairing of spatial acoustics with musical atmosphere. Ah Jie often invites Xiaoyun to play piano samples for his white noise projects.

daily_habits.activity: Listens to classical music radio stations (such as BBC Radio 3) while eating breakfast as a ritual to start the day.

daily_habits.time_pattern: Wednesday and Friday evenings, 8:00 PM - 9:30 PM
daily_habits.activity: Unwavering piano practice time, focusing primarily on Hanon finger exercises for warm-up and refining specific pieces.
Visits local independent record stores or Livehouses in Hangzhou to seek new musical inspiration.
Wears noise-canceling headphones and switches playlists based on the day's work mood (listening to minimalism when busy, and City Pop when relaxed).

emotion_music_map.behavior: Plays Bach's 'The Well-Tempered Clavier' or repetitive minimalist works (such as Philip Glass) to calm the mind through rigorous structure and recurring melodies.
Listens alone to Chopin's 'Nocturnes' or slow movements by Rachmaninoff, allowing oneself to immerse in the music's melancholy to release emotions.
Improvises freely on the piano, experimenting with dissonant chords, and immediately records melodic motifs using a mobile phone.
Revisits childhood piano practice recordings or listens to Joe Hisaishi's 'Summer,' reminiscing about summers spent with her grandfather.

life_events.event: Won the silver award at her first Hangzhou Children's Piano Competition. Although disappointed about not winning gold, her grandfather gifted her an old erhu as encouragement, teaching her that music is not just about competition but also companionship.

Provided improvisational accompaniment and sang 'Farewell' for her entire class at the graduation ceremony. This was the first time she realized music's powerful ability to unite emotions, solidifying her resolve to keep music as a lifelong companion even if she didn't become a professional musician.

At her architectural design exhibition, she boldly discarded background light music and instead performed a live piano piece composed based on the building's flow. This received high praise from the judges, achieving a perfect fusion of architecture and music for the first time.

scenes.音乐行为: A Yamaha U1 upright piano sits in the corner, with high-fidelity speakers by the window. This is her primary space for late-night practice and listening to symphonies alone.
Before presenting proposals to clients, she plays carefully selected ambient music (such as Ryuichi Sakamoto's 'Async') via a Bluetooth speaker to regulate the meeting's rhythm and atmosphere.
On pleasant weekend days, she brings a portable Bluetooth speaker, sits by the lake listening to Debussy's 'Reflets dans l'eau,' and experiences synesthesia while gazing at the real water.
Occasionally on weekend evenings, she visits MAO Livehouse to watch independent band performances, standing amidst the crowd to feel the bass vibrations and release work-related stress.
